mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present values and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is continuous and does not require a threshold crossing.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.41 percent as of t-1mo indicates an already-eased policy stance relative to prior tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following rate declines are not significantly higher than returns following rate increases (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low, this Sahm-rule-like threshold signals an inflecting labor market that historically precedes recessionary equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8 percent as of t-1mo remains near cycle lows, so trigger is not currently active
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC across triggered windows is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in RSAFS (retail sales) growth and HOUST (housing starts) growth jointly signals broad-based consumer and housing demand contraction that individually weak single-series readings do not capture, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when both decelerate together.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth turns negative while HOUST simultaneously falls below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 505969 million and HOUST at 1162 thousand units as of latest snapshot provide baseline levels for joint deceleration test
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus either single-series model.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, continued positive INDPRO growth reflects capacity utilization pressure that raises input costs and margin risk, causing the historically positive INDPRO-return relationship to weaken or reverse, so forward 60-day S&P 500 returns become less reliably positive despite rising industrial production.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.475 as of t-2mo reflects continued expansion in industrial output
failure_condition: The sign of the INDPRO-forward return relationship in late-cycle regimes matches the early-cycle sign with equal or greater Rank IC, indicating no regime-dependent attenuation.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above 4 percent, markets price in a higher probability of near-term Fed tightening, producing a short-horizon negative equity reaction over the following 5 trading days as discount rate expectations reprice abruptly.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL index at 254.148 as of t-1mo used to compute trailing YoY inflation rate
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following inflation-trigger events are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the effect size is not significant at p < 0.10 across at least 20 historical trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects resilient labor income generation that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days, independent of any threshold condition.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150816 thousand persons as of t-1mo indicates continued payroll expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and forward 20d SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS rising), a decline in HOUST is amplified because higher mortgage rates compound with falling housing demand, producing outsized negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter compared to the same HOUST decline occurring during easing regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5 percent over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1162 thousand units as of t-2mo provides current housing starts level
failure_condition: The negative return effect of HOUST declines during tightening regimes is not significantly larger than during easing regimes (interaction coefficient p > 0.10), or the sign reverses.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is rising while FEDFUNDS remains elevated (policy lag not yet reversed), the combination of deteriorating labor conditions and still-restrictive monetary policy signals imminent earnings risk not captured by either series alone, producing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS remains within 0.5pp of its cycle peak
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8 percent and FEDFUNDS at 2.41 percent as of latest snapshot establish baseline for joint trigger monitoring
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle transitioning to labor deterioration
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show forward 5d returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the interaction effect is fully explained by the UNRATE-only single-series model.
